steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to explain WHY nautical miles are used in seafaring navigation. The answer must connect nautical miles to a reason — not just describe what they are.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'nautical miles equal one minute of latitude, a feature nautical charts use to calculate distances': States the correlation but never explains why that makes nautical miles useful for navigation. It restates facts without supplying the causal reason. ✗
  • Choice B — 'nautical charts are more accurate than flat maps because they account for Earth's curvature': Compares chart types but never mentions nautical miles or why they are the chosen unit. Off-target. ✗
  • Choice C — 'since they directly correlate to coordinates on nautical charts, which account for Earth's curvature, nautical miles are an efficient way to calculate distances at sea': Completes the full chain — nautical miles match chart coordinates → those coordinates account for curvature → therefore nautical miles are efficient for sea navigation. ✓
  • Choice D — 'nautical miles account for curvature, whereas latitude and longitude do not': Directly contradicts the notes, which state that latitude/longitude coordinates DO account for Earth's curvature. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
